computation: HUF Impulse Dependency Chain Analysis — Fukushima 2011
question: Is there a causal dependency chain in the carrier impulse responses? Does closure create a forced relay?
answer: Yes. The closure constraint (sum to 1) creates a forced cascade relay.

stage_1_shock.description: Nuclear drops. Gas and Other_Fossil surge immediately. Only carriers with existing dispatchable capacity can respond.

stage_2_emergency.description: Other_Fossil hits peak. Coal begins rising. Expensive emergency response at maximum.

stage_3_handoff_1.description: Other_Fossil begins declining. Gas takes over as primary compensator. Nuclear hits absolute zero.

stage_4_handoff_2.description: Coal plateaus and begins slow decline. Solar crosses structural visibility threshold.

stage_5_long_tail.description: Gas begins slow decline. Solar continues exponential ramp. Other_Fossil collapses below pre-Fukushima baseline.

key_finding.mechanism: Closure constraint (sum to 1) forces carriers into a relay. Each absorbs share until it hits its dispatchability or infrastructure ceiling, then hands off to the next.
key_finding.sequence_determined_by: Dispatchability (can existing plants ramp?) then build time (how long to install new capacity?)
key_finding.loudspeaker_difference: In a loudspeaker, drivers are independent — each responds to its electrical band signal without affecting others. In a composition, carriers are coupled through closure — one carrier's response constrains all others. The impulse response is not parallel; it is serial relay.

implication_for_sbp: The SBP groups carriers by physics (fossil vs non-fossil), but the relay operates by response speed (fast dispatchable vs slow infrastructure). These are different organizing principles. CR detects the relay handoffs because they change which carriers are active on each side of the SBP partition.
implication_for_governance: The dependency chain IS the governance information. A policymaker seeing the relay knows: (1) emergency response will be fossil-heavy and expensive, (2) sustained compensation will be gas-dominated, (3) structural transition to renewables takes 7+ years. The time constants of the relay are the actual constraints on energy policy.
